casos.presentacion: A 58-year-old man is referred to your surgical oncology clinic following diagnosis of a 4 cm sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma. Colonoscopy confirms the lesion at 30 cm from the anal verge. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis shows no distant metastases but identifies two suspicious periaortic lymph nodes. MSI/MMR testing is pending. He is otherwise well, ECOG 0.
puntosClave: Explain the biological basis of tumour invasion and metastasis relevant to surgical decision-making
Apply R-classification (R0/R1/R2) and interpret its prognostic implications
Justify the principles of lymphadenectomy and sentinel node biopsy
Describe the rationale and sequencing of neoadjuvant and adjuvant therapy
Define the structure and evidence base for multidisciplinary tumour boards

casos.presentacion: A 62-year-old man is referred with a 2-week history of rectal bleeding and change in bowel habit. Colonoscopy identifies a 3 cm mass at 8 cm from the anal verge. Biopsy confirms adenocarcinoma. He has no significant comorbidities (ECOG 0). His wife is present and very anxious about whether he will 'need a bag'.
puntosClave: Describe the anatomical basis and surgical planes of TME
Apply NCCN 2026 staging criteria to determine neoadjuvant strategy for rectal cancer
Identify the technical steps and critical landmarks of right hemicolectomy, left hemicolectomy, and low anterior resection
Interpret MRI mesorectal staging and CRM threat assessment
Grade and manage anastomotic leak using the International Study Group classification

casos.presentacion: You are a PGY3 surgical resident who has noticed that patients undergoing emergency colostomy reversal (Hartmann's reversal) at your centre have a higher anastomotic leak rate when the reversal is performed within 3 months of the original operation compared to those operated after 6 months. You want to design a study to test this observation formally.
puntosClave: Apply the FINER criteria to evaluate the quality of a research question
Construct a PICO question from a clinical observation
Distinguish null hypothesis, alternative hypothesis, and directional hypothesis
Identify common errors in hypothesis formulation (HARKing, p-hacking, outcome switching)
Match a hypothesis to an appropriate study design

casos.presentacion: Oral-exam case on Gastric Cancer (Surgical Oncology). The examiner will pose a clinical scenario from this area. Be ready for the differential diagnosis, indicated tests, and management. Material topics: Surgical Anatomy and Lymph Node Stations; Operative Procedures; Post-Gastrectomy Syndromes; Gastric Cancer Staging and Lymphadenectomy.

Oral-exam case on Hepatopancreatic-Biliary Surgery (Surgical Oncology). The examiner will pose a clinical scenario from this area. Be ready for the differential diagnosis, indicated tests, and management. Material topics: Liver: Couinaud Anatomy and Resection Planning; Pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple Procedure); Cholangiocarcinoma; Pancreatic Cancer — Resectability Criteria & Borderline Disease.

Oral-exam case on Breast Surgery & Melanoma (Surgical Oncology). The examiner will pose a clinical scenario from this area. Be ready for the differential diagnosis, indicated tests, and management. Material topics: Breast Conservation vs Mastectomy; Sentinel Node Biopsy Technique; Molecular Subtypes and Surgical Implications; Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy — Indications & Surgical Planning.

Oral-exam case on Sarcoma & Peritoneal Oncology (Surgical Oncology). The examiner will pose a clinical scenario from this area. Be ready for the differential diagnosis, indicated tests, and management. Material topics: Retroperitoneal Sarcoma; Cytoreductive Surgery + HIPEC; PIPAC — Pressurised Intraperitoneal Aerosol Chemotherapy; Pseudomyxoma Peritonei — Histological Subtypes & CRS+HIPEC.

Oral-exam case on Endocrine Surgery (Surgical Oncology). The examiner will pose a clinical scenario from this area. Be ready for the differential diagnosis, indicated tests, and management. Material topics: Thyroid Cancer — Classification, Staging & Oncological Management; Thyroidectomy — Technique & Nerve Monitoring; Complications of Thyroid Surgery; Primary Hyperparathyroidism & Parathyroid Carcinoma.

Oral-exam case on Emergency Surgery (General Surgery). The examiner will pose a clinical scenario from this area. Be ready for the differential diagnosis, indicated tests, and management. Material topics: Acute Appendicitis; Perforated Peptic Ulcer; Small Bowel Obstruction; Damage Control Surgery.

Oral-exam case on Hernia Surgery (General Surgery). The examiner will pose a clinical scenario from this area. Be ready for the differential diagnosis, indicated tests, and management. Material topics: Anatomy of Groin Hernias; Operative Techniques; Laparoscopic Groin Hernia Repair — TEP & TAPP Technical Details; Complex Incisional Hernia.

Oral-exam case on Bariatric & Metabolic Surgery (General Surgery). The examiner will pose a clinical scenario from this area. Be ready for the differential diagnosis, indicated tests, and management. Material topics: Indications and Patient Selection; Pre-operative Assessment & Multidisciplinary Team; RYGB — Operative Steps & Anatomy; Sleeve Gastrectomy — Operative Steps & Bougie Size.

Oral-exam case on Endocrine Surgery (General Surgery). The examiner will pose a clinical scenario from this area. Be ready for the differential diagnosis, indicated tests, and management. Material topics: Thyroid Nodule and Cancer; Thyroidectomy — Technique & Nerve Monitoring; Complications of Thyroid Surgery; Primary Hyperparathyroidism.
